title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Repository Template Mission
summary: A shared frontend platform team. The team needs a reproducible library repo with formatting, correctness linting, strict typing, domain literals, and dead-code controls. Planned artifact: Deliver Strict TypeScript Library Template with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin supported runtime and package-manager versions with one authoritative source.
Enable rules for invalid control flow, unsafe APIs, promises, hooks, and accessibility as applicable.
Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-safe-removal-efwo2c: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Removal into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-safe-removal-efwo2c:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

stimulus.title: Safe Removal failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Safe Removal condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Safe Removal applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
